behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver -> dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input handoff, not one isolated function. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only parser logic can miss dropped transitive nodes during handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network/subprocess required, no AI output, no agentic chain (integration-brake passes). Adversarial critique: if extraction and inclusion are implemented in one pure function, Unit could be enough. Runner-up: Unit; Integration wins because behavior text explicitly requires both extraction and inclusion in the check (cross-module handoff). Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real API/network; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest parsing and dependency-resolution modules with fixture manifests containing nested/transitive deps; wire into the vulnerability-check request builder with a fake in-process vuln client, and assert every expected direct+transitive package/version is present in emitted check inputs.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This targets contract wiring between version-range extraction, query construction, and response matching/filtering. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing query builder alone can miss mismatch between built range and matcher logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/subprocess needed; API can be stubbed deterministically; no AI output; no multi-agent chain (integration-brake passes). Adversarial critique: if the only bug class is malformed HTTP query params against real API semantics, System may be needed. Runner-up: System; Integration wins because behavior text focuses on version-range correctness, which can be reproduced with controlled in-process API fixtures. Rejection-gate: rejected System due to no explicit requirement for real network/API behavior in text; rejected Agentic/Workflow due to no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end within process: parse manifest ranges, build vuln query, feed stubbed vuln DB responses containing same package with multiple ranges, and assert only CVEs matching the manifest’s exact version constraints are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure ordering logic on in-memory CVE records; failure boundary is a single comparator/sort function. Adversarial critique: if severity labels are normalized in another module before sort, an Integration test could be needed. Runner-up: Integration; Unit wins because behavior text is strictly about ordering semantics, not module handoff or I/O. Rejection-gate: rejected higher levels since text contains no OS/network/subprocess or AI/model dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sort function with mixed severities and equal-severity CVEs with different CVSS values; assert exact output order including tie-break behavior.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears in parse/edit/serialize roundtrip across modules (e.g., unintended rewrites), not just in one mutation function. MOCK-MISS: unit test on mutation object can miss serializer side effects that alter unrelated entries. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: can run in-process using strings/files in temp fixture; no real network or AI required; no agentic chain (integration-brake passes). Adversarial critique: if updater operates on immutable map and exact serializer is out of scope, Unit could suffice. Runner-up: Unit; Integration wins because behavior explicitly requires unchanged non-target entries after manifest modification (roundtrip boundary). Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require OS-specific file events or subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real manifest parser/editor/writer pipeline on fixture manifests; update one dependency; assert resulting manifest differs only at the targeted version field and all other dependency entries are byte- or AST-equivalent.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository PR creation, which crosses real git/hosting API/network boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git host client can pass while auth, branch, or diff presentation fails in real repo. Adversarial critique: if scope were only payload construction, Integration would be enough. Runner-up: Integration; System wins because text says "created on the real repository" and validates real diff. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic/Workflow since no LLM decision chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a disposable real test repository (and real PR endpoint), trigger one vulnerable dependency bump, then assert PR exists remotely and diff contains exactly one dependency version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic semver classification logic within one function/object boundary. Adversarial critique: if version source normalization is external and error-prone, Integration may be needed. Runner-up: Integration; Unit wins because behavior text is specifically semver comparison correctness, not wiring. Rejection-gate: rejected higher levels because no real infra or AI dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator/classifier with representative version pairs (major/minor/patch/pre-release) and assert breaking-change flag is true only for major bumps per project semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration: semver decision -> PR service suppression -> report/output flag propagation across modules. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing decision logic alone misses accidental PR side-effect calls or missing output flag. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess if PR client is in-process spy/fake; no AI output; no multi-agentic sequence. Adversarial critique: if only real git host behavior can prove PR absence, System may be needed. Runner-up: System; Integration wins because behavior is control-flow gating and output surfacing, which is verifiable with wired modules and a spy PR adapter. Rejection-gate: rejected System due to behavior text not requiring real remote verification; rejected Agentic/Workflow due to no model-dependent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner pipeline with real break-detection and reporting modules plus a spy/fake PR gateway; feed a breaking bump; assert PR gateway was not invoked and scanner output includes the review-required flag.
